regional_notes: Eastern/Northern: early-maturing and drought-tolerant varieties.
Central: high-yield hybrids with good storage quality.
Western highlands: manage for cooler, wetter conditions.
varietal_notes: Use varieties recommended for your agro-ecological zone.
Confirm current releases with district extension offices or NARO.
symptoms: Fall armyworm: windowed leaves, frass in whorl.
Maize streak virus: yellow streaks, stunting.
Stem borers: dead heart, entry holes with frass.
advice: Plant at onset of rains with 75 cm x 25-30 cm spacing.
Apply compost/manure and top-dress with urea 3-4 weeks after emergence.
Scout weekly and remove egg masses early.

regional_notes: Highlands: climbing beans with staking.
Lower elevations: bush beans for shorter cycles.
varietal_notes: Select varieties recommended for your zone.
Confirm current releases with district extension offices or NARO.
symptoms: Angular leaf spot: brown angular lesions, defoliation.
Root rots: yellowing, stunting, poor roots.
Bean fly: stem lesions near soil line.
advice: Plant at onset of rains with 50 cm x 10-15 cm spacing.
Weed at 2-3 weeks and before flowering.
Rotate with cereals to reduce disease pressure.

regional_notes: Northern/Eastern: early-maturing and drought-tolerant varieties.
Central: high-yield varieties for fresh and processing markets.
Western highlands: manage erosion and waterlogging.
varietal_notes: Confirm recommended varieties with district extension offices or NARO.
symptoms: Cassava mosaic disease: mottled leaves, stunting.
Cassava brown streak: chlorosis on leaves, brown root necrosis.
Mealybugs: cottony masses, stunting.
Green mites: yellow speckling and leaf drop.
advice: Use healthy cuttings and plant at onset of rains.
Rogue infected plants early.
Spacing: 1 m x 1 m (adjust for intercropping).

regional_notes: Northern/Eastern: drought-tolerant varieties and early planting.
Central: manage humidity to reduce leaf spot pressure.
varietal_notes: Use varieties recommended for your zone.
Confirm current releases with district extension offices or NARO.
symptoms: Leaf spot: brown spots with yellow halos.
Aphids: curling leaves, sticky honeydew.
Aflatoxin risk: moldy kernels from poor drying.
advice: Plant at start of rains with 45-60 cm x 10-15 cm spacing.
Weed early; avoid disturbing pegs after flowering.
Dry quickly and store in dry conditions.

regional_notes: Lake basin and central areas: favorable moisture.
Western highlands: cooler temperatures, manage disease pressure.
varietal_notes: Select varieties recommended for your zone.
Confirm current releases with district extension offices or research stations.
symptoms: Banana weevil: damaged corms, weak plants.
Xanthomonas wilt: yellowing, bacterial ooze.
Nematodes: reduced root mass, poor anchorage.
advice: Use clean planting material and apply mulch.
Spacing around 2.5 m x 2.5 m for matooke.
Remove and destroy infected plants.

regional_notes: Arabica: eastern and western highlands.
Robusta: central and western lowlands.
varietal_notes: Use recommended varieties for altitude and zone.
Confirm with UCDA or district extension offices.
symptoms: Coffee wilt: sudden wilting and death.
Coffee berry disease: black lesions on berries.
Leaf rust: orange powdery spots.
advice: Plant at onset of rains with 2.5 m x 2.5 m spacing.
Mulch and apply organic manure.
Prune regularly and remove diseased branches.

regional_notes: Northern/Eastern: early-maturing and drought-tolerant types.
Western highlands: manage for cooler conditions and bird pressure.
varietal_notes: Choose drought-tolerant varieties for drier zones.
Confirm recommended varieties with district extension offices.
symptoms: Striga: stunted plants and poor grain fill.
Smut: black powdery heads.
Bird damage: missing grain heads near harvest.
advice: Plant at onset of rains with 60-75 cm x 15-20 cm spacing.
Weed early to reduce Striga.
Harvest when panicles are dry and grain is hard.

regional_notes: Northern/Eastern: drought-tolerant types perform well.
Highlands: finger millet with good disease tolerance.
varietal_notes: Choose varieties suitable to your zone and market use.
symptoms: Blast: gray/brown lesions on leaves and heads.
Birds: grain loss near harvest.
advice: Plant at onset of rains with 45-60 cm spacing.
Harvest promptly to reduce bird damage.

regional_notes: Eastern lowlands: lowland rice is common.
Western/Northern uplands: upland varieties may be preferred.
varietal_notes: Choose lowland or upland varieties based on field type.
symptoms: Rice blast: lesions on leaves and panicles.
Stem borers: dead hearts in vegetative stage.
advice: Plant at onset of rains or transplant for lowlands.
Maintain proper water management in lowlands.

regional_notes: Eastern/Northern: drought-tolerant varieties favored.
Central: varieties for fresh markets and processing.
varietal_notes: Orange-fleshed varieties preferred for nutrition where available.
symptoms: Sweet potato weevil: holes and tunnels in roots.
Virus diseases: leaf distortion and stunting.
advice: Plant vines at onset of rains with 75-90 cm spacing.
Harvest on time to reduce weevil damage.
